name: Boucle de Saint-Palais sur le GR65
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Saint-Palais are situated in the French Basque Country, offering a mountainous environment in the Pyrenean foothills. The terrain features significant ascents and descents, with diverse landscapes including open mountain tops, loamy forest runs, and technical sections. River valleys and charming villages characterize the region, providing varied backdrops for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Saint-Palais The most popular mountain bike trail is Boucle de Saint-Palais sur le GR65, a difficult 36.7 miles (59.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging experience through varied terrain with substantial elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Chapelle de Soyarza – Gibraltar Stele loop from Amendeuix-Oneix, a difficult 28.1 miles (45.2 km) path. This trail navigates the Pyrenean foothills, providing panoramic views and demanding sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Col d'Iparlatze – Scenic Country Road loop from Saint-Palais, a 21.4 miles (34.5 km) trail leading through scenic country roads and mountain passes, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Mountain biking around Saint-Palais is defined by Pyrenean foothills, Basque mountains, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging multi-hour rides to routes with varied landscapes. The routes in Saint-Palais are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Saint-Palais's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Saint-Palais?
A: There are &lt;em&gt;over 10 mountain bike trails&lt;/em&gt; around Saint-Palais in the French Basque Country, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes to explore the Pyrenean foothills.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Saint-Palais?
A: The trails around Saint-Palais are situated in the Pyrenean foothills, offering a mountainous environment with significant ascents and descents. You'll encounter diverse landscapes, including open mountain tops, loamy forest runs, and technical sections, characteristic of the Basque mountains and river valleys.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Saint-Palais?
A: Yes, Saint-Palais offers several routes suitable for families. While many trails involve significant elevation, there are 3 easy and 6 moderate routes that can be more accessible. It's always best to check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I might see along the mountain bike trails?
A: As you ride the trails around Saint-Palais, you can encounter several interesting landmarks and natural features. These include the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ostabat&lt;/a&gt;, the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3402827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Legend&lt;/a&gt;, and the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/930773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapelle de Soyarza&lt;/a&gt; and traverse the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Iparlatze&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the Pyrenean foothills.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Saint-Palais are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969099686/boucle-de-saint-palais-sur-le-gr65" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boucle de Saint-Palais sur le GR65&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26216182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Palais – Saint-Palais loop from Saint-Palais&lt;/a&gt; are excellent circular options.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Saint-Palais?
A: The Pyrenean foothills around Saint-Palais offer diverse conditions. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for mountain biking. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher altitudes, making some trails more challenging or inaccessible. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Saint-Palais?
A: The mountain bike trails around Saint-Palais are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the challenging terrain, the stunning views of the Basque mountains, and the diverse landscapes that range from open mountain tops to loamy forest runs.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty mountain bike trails for intermediate riders?
A: Absolutely. Saint-Palais offers 6 mountain bike trails classified as moderate difficulty, perfect for intermediate riders looking for a good challenge without the extreme demands of difficult routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37578332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele – Chapelle de Soyarza loop from Amendeuix-Oneix&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 22.0 km with a significant but manageable elevation gain.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, the mountainous environment of the Pyrenean foothills around Saint-Palais provides numerous opportunities for panoramic views. Trails like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20521420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapelle de Soyarza – Gibraltar Stele loop from Amendeuix-Oneix&lt;/a&gt; navigate these foothills, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding Basque mountains and countryside.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging mountain bike rides?
A: For experienced riders seeking a significant challenge, Saint-Palais is an excellent starting point. The region is a gateway to the 'Trans Basque' (Grande Traversée du Pays Basque), an epic multi-day adventure. For a single-day challenging ride, consider the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969099686/boucle-de-saint-palais-sur-le-gr65" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boucle de Saint-Palais sur le GR65&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 60 km with over 1400 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What is the average elevation gain on the more difficult mountain bike trails?
A: The more difficult mountain bike trails around Saint-Palais feature substantial elevation gains, reflecting the mountainous Pyrenean foothills. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969099686/boucle-de-saint-palais-sur-le-gr65" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boucle de Saint-Palais sur le GR65&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of over 1400 meters, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20521420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapelle de Soyarza – Gibraltar Stele loop from Amendeuix-Oneix&lt;/a&gt; climbs over 1200 meters, ensuring a challenging workout.
tips: The GR65 and the Great Crossing of the Basque Country by mountain bike pass right next to the chapel of Soyarza. The ascent and descent are both steep and rocky. But you will be rewarded with a beautiful panoramic view at an altitude of 290 meters.

The Ipharlatze pass (or Iparlatze pass, in Basque Iparlatzeko lekoa) is a pass located between Lantabat and Ostabat-Asme in Lower Navarre in the Pyrénées-Atlantiques. It separates the Lantabat valley from that of Bidouze.
